verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase — predominantly soluble and inner-leaflet-anchored via N-terminal myristoylation. However, SRC has documented surface pools in multiple documented contexts: (1) cancer cells display SRC on the outer leaflet ('ecto-SRC'), where it phosphorylates extracellular substrates and supports tumor progression — this is a well-established cancer-cell-restricted ecto-presentation of a nominally intracellular kinase; (2) activation-induced translocation during immune-cell and neuronal signaling moves SRC to membrane-proximal and junctional PM compartments; (3) juxtamembrane signaling at receptor tyrosine kinases and integrins positions SRC at the inner leaflet facing the cytoplasm, with regulated accessibility to extracellular-domain-proximal substrates in specialized signaling geometries. The cancer-cell ecto-pool is the key discriminator — SRC qualifies as contextual under cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation) surface display. Baseline cytoplasmic localization and canonical myristoylation anchor SRC to the inner leaflet under non-transformed conditions, but the documented cancer ecto-pool and activation-induced junctional repositioning prevent a `no` verdict.
